response.justification: Bogdan Szabo's career is overwhelmingly oriented toward web, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang development. Professional C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development the role mandates. There is no demonstrable fixed-income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain knowledge, and the only adjacent reference (RIDE Capital) is a German retail investment fintech with a Node/GraphQL stack, not a capital-markets trading system. SQL Server and Oracle experience are absent; MongoDB is the primary database in evidence. The candidate does not satisfy the mandatory technical criteria or the required domain criteria.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance, low-latency systems work at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second, async I/O, real-time metadata)
Strong software-craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and pairing in professional settings
Breadth of language exposure (Java, C, C++, DLang, TypeScript) and prior Java work suggest adaptability to compiled, statically-typed environments
concerns: C++ experience is effectively ~6 months (National Instruments, 2014–2015) — a 10x shortfall against the 5–10 year requirement, with no Windows-platform C++ work evident beyond that role
Zero fixed-income, derivatives, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain knowledge; RIDE Capital is a retail fintech product, not a capital-markets system
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database work is predominantly MongoDB and generic SQL, with no evidence of enterprise RDBMS proficiency required for the role
